feed.title: Hawaiʻi County Bill Tracker — all updates

entry.title: Bill 132 (Hawaiʻi County): PCPWMT: Withdrawn - 05/19/26
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 6 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED) BY ADDING AN ARTICLE RELATING TO PAID PARKING FACILITIES IN KAILUA VILLAGE

Adds a new article to regulate parking rates at private parking facilities in Kailua Village.

Latest action: PCPWMT: Withdrawn - 05/19/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 155 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the Federal Grants – HOME Investment Partnership- American Rescue Plan Program account ($1,977,568)

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 155 passes second & final reading - 05/20/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 151 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-46, AS AMENDED, RELATING TO PUBLIC IMPROVEMENTS AND FINANCING THEREOF FOR THE FISCAL YEAR JULY 1, 2025, TO JUNE 30, 2026

Adds the Department of Public Works – Safe Routes to Waiākea School – Kīlauea Avenue Part 1 project ($1,386,656) to the Capital Budget. Funds for this project shall be provided from State Grants Receivable.

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 151 passes second & final reading - 05/20/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 150 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the State Grants – Safe Routes to School – Non-Infrastructure account ($50,344)

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 150 passes second & final reading - 05/20/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 148 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
DESIGNATES TAX MAP KEY: 6-6-001:010 AS “LAʻELAʻE PARK”, AMENDS CHAPTER 15, ARTICLE 8, SECTION 15-68.1, AND AMENDS CHAPTER 14, ARTICLE 1, SECTION 14-1 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO PARKS AND RECREATIONAL FACILITIES

Adds Laʻelaʻe Park, located in Waimea, to the Parks and Recreational Facility Schedule and to Section 14-1 of the County Code, which prohibits the consumption of alcohol at any time.

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 148 passes second & final reading - 05/20/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 153 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the State Grants – Coffee Berry Borer/Coffee Leaf Rust Pesticide Subsidy Program account ($150,000)

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 153 passes second & final reading - 06/03/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 66 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
ADOPTS THE COUNTY OF HAWAI‘I GENERAL PLAN 2045 AND REPEALS ORDINANCE NO. 05-025, AS AMENDED

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: PCPLUED: Bill 66, Draft 2 postponed to May 4, 2026 - 04/07/26 PCPLUED-10: Recommended passage on first reading - 05/04/26 Council: Bill 66, Draft 2 passes first reading; adopts PCPLUED-10 - 05/20/26 Council: Bill 66, Draft 2 passes second & final reading - 06/03/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 159 (Hawaiʻi County): Council: Bill 159 withdrawn by introducer - 06/03/26
entry.content:
INITIATES AN AMENDMENT TO ARTICLES III, V, VI, VII, VIII, AND X OF THE CHARTER OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI (2024 EDITION), RELATING TO THE ESTABLISHMENT OF A BIENNIAL OPERATING BUDGET AND CAPITAL BUDGET

Establishes biennial budgeting for the County, with an initial primary budget to be adopted every odd-numbered year and a supplemental operating budget to be adopted every even-numbered year.

Latest action: Council: Bill 159 withdrawn by introducer - 06/03/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 158 (Hawaiʻi County): Council: Bill 158 amended to Draft 2 - 06/03/26
entry.content:
INITIATES AN AMENDMENT TO ARTICLES III, IV, V, VI, VII, VIII, X, XIII, AND XVI, OF THE CHARTER OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAI‘I (2024 EDITION), RELATING TO THE STRUCTURE AND ORGANIZATION OF THE COUNTY

Converts the County’s government to a hybrid council-manager system, in which certain County departments and operations are overseen by an appointed County Manager and others are overseen by an elected Mayor.

Latest action: Council: Bill 158 amended to Draft 2 - 06/03/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 136 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
RELATES TO PUBLIC IMPROVEMENTS AND FINANCING THEREOF FOR THE FISCAL YEAR JULY 1, 2026, TO JUNE 30, 2027

Draft 3 requires a total appropriation of $380,200,000, of which $55,700,000 are intended to be funded by Federal Grants, $23,000,000 to be funded by State Grants, $292,500,000 to be funded in whole or part by bonds, $8,500,000 to be funded by private funds, and $500,000 to be funded by the Public Access, Open Space, and Natural Resources Preservation Maintenance Fund.

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 136, Draft 4 passes second & final reading - 06/04/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 135 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
ESTABLISHES AN OPERATING BUDGET FOR THE COUNTY OF HAWAI‘I FOR THE FISCAL YEAR JULY 1, 2026, TO JUNE 30, 2027

Draft 3 includes estimated revenues of $976,408,620 and appropriations of $975,980,388.

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 135, Draft 4 passes second & final reading - 06/04/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 126 (Hawaiʻi County): PCPLUED: Referred to Planning Director & Windward Planning Commission - 02/03/26
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 25, ARTICLES 1, 3, 5, AND 7, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO SPECIAL DISTRICTS

Creates the Downtown Pāhoa commercial district as a new special zoning district; establishes new permitted uses.

Latest action: PCPLUED: Referred to Planning Director & Windward Planning Commission - 02/03/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 125 (Hawaiʻi County): PCPLUED: Referred to Planning Director & Windward Planning Commission - 02/03/26
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 25, ARTICLE 7, DIVISION 4, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO THE PĀHOA VILLAGE DESIGN DISTRICT

Clarifies duties, procedures, and other regulations regarding the Pāhoa Village Design Guidelines and the Pāhoa Design Review Committee; makes other technical and non-substantive amendments.

Latest action: PCPLUED: Referred to Planning Director & Windward Planning Commission - 02/03/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 124 (Hawaiʻi County): PCPLUED: Referred to Planning Director & Windward Planning Commission - 02/03/26
entry.content:
AMENDS SECTION 25-8-26 (PĀHOA ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED) BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM VILLAGE COMMERCIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (CV-10) AND SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-10) TO DOWNTOWN PĀHOA COMMERCIAL (DPC) AT WAIAKAHIʻULA, PUNA, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY THE AREA AS MORE PARTICULARLY DESCRIBED HEREIN

Rezones certain areas in Pāhoa to a prospective special zoning district classification to be known as the Downtown Pāhoa Commercial District, which is proposed for establishment via

Latest action: PCPLUED: Referred to Planning Director & Windward Planning Commission - 02/03/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 164 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Postponed
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 2, ARTICLE 13, DIVISION 2, SECTION 2-69, OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO THE HOUSING ADMINISTRATOR

Requires Council confirmation for the Housing Administrator.

Status: — → Postponed

Latest action: GOEAC: Postponed to June 16, 2026 - 06/02/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 163 (Hawaiʻi County): FC: Amended to Draft 2 - 6/16/2026
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 19, ARTICLE 8, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO REAL PROPERTY TAXES

Establishes a new real property tax dedication for certain properties that are owned by a descendant of a person who owned the property at least one hundred years ago to be subject to the minimum tax.

Latest action: FC: Amended to Draft 2 - 6/16/2026

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 167 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed First Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the Federal Grants – Justice Assistance Grant-Special Response Team Capability Enhancement Grant account ($35,023)

Status: — → Passed First Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 167 passes first reading - 06/03/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 166 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed First Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-50, AS AMENDED, AN ORDINANCE AMENDING CHAPTER 6 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO TRANSIENT ACCOMMODATIONS

Delays the effective date of Ordinance No. 25-50, relating to transient accommodations, until September 1, 2026.

Status: — → Passed First Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 166 passes first reading - 06/03/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 161 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed First Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the Fund Balance From Previous Year - Reserved account ($443,775.57)

Status: — → Passed First Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 161 passes first reading; adopts FC-155 - 06/03/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 160 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed First Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the Real Property Tax Penalties account ($1,125,000)

Status: — → Passed First Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 160 passes first reading; adopts FC-154 - 06/03/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 156 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed First Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 2, ARTICLE 25, SECTION 2-137, OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO APPROPRIATION OF FUNDS TO NONPROFIT ORGANIZATIONS

Requires a nonprofit organization applying for County grant funds to certify, under penalty of perjury, that the organization has internal policies relating to management, audits, fiscal procedures, nepotism, and conflicts of interest.

Status: — → Passed First Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 156 passes first reading; adopts GOEAC-133 - 06/03/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 152 (Hawaiʻi County): Council: Bill 152 passes second of three required readings - 06/03/26
entry.content:
INITIATES AN AMENDMENT TO ARTICLE III, SECTION 3-18, OF THE CHARTER OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAI‘I (2024 EDITION), RELATING TO THE OFFICE OF THE COUNTY AUDITOR

Clarifies that the County Auditor has the same power with respect to employees within the Office of the County Auditor as department heads in the Executive Branch and specifies that the County Auditor may appoint a deputy.

Latest action: Council: Bill 152 passes second of three required readings - 06/03/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 165 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed First Reading
entry.content:
INITIATES AN AMENDMENT TO ARTICLE X OF THE CHARTER OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAI‘I (2024 EDITION), RELATING TO PUBLIC ACCESS, OPEN SPACE, AND NATURAL RESOURCES PRESERVATION

Increases the annual allocation dedicated to the Public Access, Open Space, and Natural Resources Preservation maintenance fund from 0.25 percent to 0.5 percent of real property tax revenue; removes the accrual limit for the fund; provides that all expenses from the fund must align with the fund’s purpose

Status: — → Passed First Reading

Latest action: GOEAC-139: Recommended passage on first reading - 06/02/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 162 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed First Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 3 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO PAID PARKING FACILITIES

Adds a new article to establish signage requirements for paid parking facilities, to include a schedule of rates, notice of any additional fees, hours of operation, and information on towing procedures.

Status: — → Passed First Reading

Latest action: PCPWMT-13: Recommended passage on first reading - 06/02/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 157 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed First Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 7 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO DISASTER RECOVERY

Establishes a County Disaster Recovery Program to coordinate short- and long-term recovery efforts following major disasters, led by a recovery officer appointed by the mayor with the responsibility for recovery planning, interagency coordination, community engagement, funding management, and the development of interim and long-term recovery and resilience plans.

Status: — → Passed First Reading

Latest action: PCHSW-09: Recommended passage on first reading - 06/02/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 2985 (Kauaʻi): In Committee → —
entry.content:
A BILL FOR AN ORDINANCE AMENDING CHAPTER 6, KAUA‘I COUNTY CODE 1987, AS AMENDED, RELATING TO THE COUNTY HOUSING DEVELOPMENT FUND

Status: In Committee → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-01

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 2989 (Kauaʻi): On agenda 2026-05-27
entry.content:
A BILL FOR AN ORDINANCE RELATING TO CAPITAL IMPROVEMENTS AND FINANCING THEREOF FOR THE FISCAL YEAR JULY 1, 2026 TO JUNE 30, 2027 (Fiscal Year 2026-2027 CIP Budget)

entry.title: Bill 2988 (Kauaʻi): On agenda 2026-05-27
entry.content:
A BILL FOR AN ORDINANCE RELATING TO THE OPERATING BUDGET AND FINANCING THEREOF FOR THE FISCAL YEAR JULY 1, 2026 TO JUNE 30, 2027 (Fiscal Year 2026-2027 Operating Budget)

Latest action: On agenda 2026-05-27

View on the council site

entry.title: Resolution 2026-11 (Kauaʻi): — → In Committee
entry.content:
RESOLUTION ESTABLISHING THE REAL PROPERTY TAX RATES FOR THE FISCAL YEAR JULY 1, 2026 TO JUNE 30, 2027 FOR THE COUNTY OF KAUA‘I

Status: — → In Committee

Latest action: On agenda 2026-06-03

Increases the annual allocation dedicated to the Public Access, Open Space, and Natural Resources Preservation maintenance fund from 0.25 percent to 0.5 percent of real property tax revenue; removes the accrual limit for the fund; provides that all expenses from the fund must align with the fund’s purpose

entry.title: Bill 162 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed First Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 3 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO PAID PARKING FACILITIES

Adds a new article to establish signage requirements for paid parking facilities, to include a schedule of rates, notice of any additional fees, hours of operation, and information on towing procedures.

Status: Passed First Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-06-17

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 157 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed First Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 7 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO DISASTER RECOVERY

Establishes a County Disaster Recovery Program to coordinate short- and long-term recovery efforts following major disasters, led by a recovery officer appointed by the mayor with the responsibility for recovery planning, interagency coordination, community engagement, funding management, and the development of interim and long-term recovery and resilience plans.

Status: Passed First Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-06-17

View on the council site

entry.title: New: RES26-146 (Honolulu) — INTRO
entry.content:
AUTHORIZING THE DIRECTOR OF BUDGET AND FISCAL SERVICES TO ISSUE AND SELL IN ONE OR MORE SERIES NOT TO EXCEED $11,000,000 PRINCIPAL AMOUNT OF GENERAL OBLIGATION BONDS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF HONOLULU FOR FINANCING THE AMOUNT APPROPRIATED IN ORDINANCE NO. 26-_ (ALSO REFERRED TO, UPON ITS APPROVAL, AS BILL 23 (2026), CD2, FD1, AND AS MAY BE FURTHER AMENDED) OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF HONOLULU FOR THE PURPOSE OF FINANCING CAPITAL COSTS OF THE HONOLULU RAIL TRANSIT PROJECT.

Recommendation of the adoption of the Resolution authorizing the issuance and sale not to exceed principal amount of General Obligation Bonds to finance the capital costs of the Honolulu Rail Transit Project (“HRTP”); Capital Budget Ordinance for Fiscal Year July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2027; Honolulu Authority for Rapid Transportation (“HART’); Budget and Fiscal Services.

Latest action: Introduced.

View on the council site

entry.title: New: RES26-145 (Honolulu) — INTRO
entry.content:
AUTHORIZING THE DIRECTOR OF BUDGET AND FISCAL SERVICES TO ISSUE AND SELL IN ONE OR MORE SERIES NOT TO EXCEED $205,000,000 PRINCIPAL AMOUNT OF GENERAL OBLIGATION BONDS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF HONOLULU FOR FINANCING THE AMOUNTS APPROPRIATED IN CERTAIN CAPITAL BUDGET ORDINANCES OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF HONOLULU SPECIFIED IN SUCH CAPITAL BUDGET ORDINANCES.

Recommendation of the adoption of the Resolution authorizing the issuance and sale of not to exceed principal amount of General Obligation Bonds; Capital Budget Ordinance for Fiscal Year July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2027; Budget and Fiscal Services.

Latest action: Introduced.

View on the council site

entry.title: New: RES26-144 (Honolulu) — INTRO
entry.content:
AUTHORIZING THE ADOPTION OF THE AGENCY-SPECIFIC RECORDS RETENTION SCHEDULE FOR THE HONOLULU LIQUOR COMMISSION OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF HONOLULU.

Latest action: Introduced.

View on the council site

entry.title: New: RES26-148 (Honolulu) — INTRO
entry.content:
ACCEPTING A GIFT OF $6,750.67 TO THE CITY FROM SHINNYO-EN HAWAIʻI FOR THE UPKEEP AND MAINTENANCE OF ALA MOANA REGIONAL PARK.

Latest action: Introduced.

View on the council site

entry.title: New: RES26-147 (Honolulu) — INTRO
entry.content:
ACCEPTING A GIFT OF TRANSPORTATION, LODGING, MEALS, AND REGISTRATION FEE, VALUED AT $5,600, TO THE CITY FROM THE NATIONAL ASSOCIATION OF STATE PROCUREMENT OFFICIALS (NASPO) FOR TWO STAFF MEMBERS FROM THE CITY AND COUNTY OF HONOLULU TO ATTEND THE NASPO REACH CONFERENCE AND TRAINING EVENT IN OKLAHOMA CITY, OKLAHOMA, FROM JULY 21-25, 2026.

Latest action: Introduced.

View on the council site

entry.title: Resolution 2026-11 (Kauaʻi): In Committee → —
entry.content:
RESOLUTION ESTABLISHING THE REAL PROPERTY TAX RATES FOR THE FISCAL YEAR JULY 1, 2026 TO JUNE 30, 2027 FOR THE COUNTY OF KAUA‘I

Status: In Committee → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-09
